_comment: PhantomNet Sprint Config — Week 15 (Month 1 Week 3: Polish & Hardening, Days 11-15)
milestone_description: Core Sentinel Layer: ATT&CK mapping, Snort/Sigma rules, Jinja2 playbooks, enhanced STIX, SentinelPlaybook DB, Sentinel Dashboard with approve/reject/export.

tasks.Title: End-to-End Test #1 — SSH Brute Force Scenario
tasks.Objective: Manually insert test PacketLogs simulating SSH brute force and verify sentinel generates correct T1110.001 playbook with all expected fields.
Tasks: Insert test PacketLogs simulating SSH brute force traffic (port 2222, repeated auth failures)
Trigger sentinel pipeline to process the injected data
Verify sentinel generates a playbook with MITRE technique T1110.001 (Brute Force: Password Guessing)
Validate Snort rule contains correct msg, flow, threshold, and classtype for SSH brute force
Validate Sigma rule has correct logsource and detection block for SSH auth failures
Verify STIX bundle contains correct ExternalReferences to ATT&CK T1110.001
Verify playbook Markdown renders all 7 sections (Header, Summary, IOC Table, ATT&CK Mapping, Containment Steps, Artifacts, metadata)
Document the test scenario and expected output in test report
Deliverables: Documented SSH brute force test scenario with expected vs actual output
Verified T1110.001 playbook generation — all sections correct
Test evidence screenshots/logs saved to docs/
Warnings: **Do NOT** modify existing sentinel_service.py logic — this is validation only.
Use realistic test data that mimics actual honeypot SSH traffic patterns.

tasks.Title: End-to-End Test #2 — SQL Injection Scenario
tasks.Objective: Insert HTTP PacketLogs with SQLi payloads and verify T1190 playbook + Snort rule are generated correctly.
Tasks: Insert test PacketLogs with SQL injection payloads (port 8080, HTTP requests with SQLi patterns)
Trigger sentinel pipeline to process the injected SQLi traffic
Verify playbook maps to MITRE technique T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)
Verify Snort rule has correct msg and classtype for SQL injection
Validate Sigma rule detection logic for HTTP SQLi patterns
Verify playbook uses sqli_attempt.md.j2 template with correct containment steps (WAF review, input validation audit, DB integrity check)
Document test results with evidence
Deliverables: Documented SQLi test scenario with expected vs actual output
Verified T1190 playbook + Snort rule correctness
Test evidence saved to docs/

tasks.Title: End-to-End Test #3 — Port Scan Scenario
tasks.Objective: Insert multi-port PacketLogs simulating port scanning and verify T1046 playbook is generated with correct Sigma detection logic.
Tasks: Insert test PacketLogs simulating port scanning (multiple destination ports from single source IP)
Trigger sentinel pipeline to process the injected port scan traffic
Verify playbook maps to MITRE technique T1046 (Network Service Discovery)
Verify Sigma rule has correct detection logic for port scan patterns
Verify playbook uses port_scan.md.j2 template with correct containment steps (network segmentation review, exposed service audit)
Validate STIX bundle correctness for T1046
Document test results with evidence
Deliverables: Documented port scan test scenario with expected vs actual output
Verified T1046 playbook + Sigma rule correctness
Test evidence saved to docs/

tasks.Title: UI Polish — Loading States, Error Handling, Empty States
tasks.Objective: Add spinners during API calls, error toasts on failure, and empty state display when no playbooks exist. Follow existing dashboard patterns.
Tasks: Add loading spinner/skeleton to SentinelDashboard during playbook list fetch
Add error toast/notification component on API call failures
Add empty state display when no playbooks exist (icon + message + CTA)
Add loading state to PlaybookViewer when fetching playbook details
Add loading state to ApprovalControls during approve/reject API calls
Ensure error handling follows existing dashboard patterns (MetricCard, ThreatFeed, etc.)
Test all loading/error/empty states manually
Deliverables: Updated SentinelDashboard.jsx with loading states
Error toast/notification component integrated
Empty state component for no playbooks scenario
All Sentinel components have proper loading/error handling

tasks.Title: Fix Pipeline Bugs from Day 11 E2E Tests
tasks.Objective: Fix all pipeline bugs discovered during Day 11 end-to-end tests. Prioritize: data flow issues, missing fields, DB save failures. All 3 scenarios must produce correct output.
Tasks: Triage all bugs found during Day 11 SSH brute force, SQLi, and port scan tests
Fix data flow issues — ensure campaign cluster data correctly flows to sentinel_service
Fix missing fields — ensure all 21 SentinelPlaybook columns are populated
Fix DB save failures — ensure SentinelPlaybook records persist correctly
Re-run all 3 E2E test scenarios and verify correct output
Document all fixes applied with before/after evidence
Deliverables: All 3 E2E scenarios (SSH brute force, SQLi, port scan) produce correct output
Bug fix documentation with before/after evidence
Updated sentinel_service.py or related files as needed
Warnings: **Priority order**: P0 (blocks demo) → P1 (wrong output) → P2 (cosmetic).
Do NOT add new features — this is bug-fix only.

tasks.Title: Validate Snort Rule Syntax Correctness
tasks.Objective: Validate all generated Snort rules follow Snort 2.9/3.0 syntax correctly. Manual review or parser-based validation.
Tasks: Collect all Snort rules generated by the sentinel pipeline
Verify correct semicolon placement in rule options
Verify correct parentheses usage in rule body
Verify proper escape characters in msg and content fields
Verify SID uniqueness across all generated rules
Verify classtype values match Snort standard classtypes
Verify ATT&CK reference URLs are correctly formatted
Document any syntax issues found and fix in rule_generator.py
Deliverables: Snort rule syntax validation report
All rules follow Snort 2.9/3.0 syntax correctly
Fixes applied to rule_generator.py if needed

tasks.Title: Validate Sigma Rules with YAML Parser
tasks.Objective: Validate all generated Sigma rules are valid YAML with correct Sigma schema. Verify logsource structure, detection block, and tag format.
Tasks: Collect all Sigma rules generated by the sentinel pipeline
Parse each rule with a YAML parser to verify valid YAML syntax
Verify logsource structure has correct category/product/service fields
Verify detection block follows Sigma schema (selection, condition)
Verify tags follow ATT&CK format (attack.t1110, attack.credential_access, etc.)
Verify level field matches severity (critical/high/medium/low)
Document any schema issues found and fix in rule_generator.py
Deliverables: Sigma rule schema validation report
All rules are valid YAML with correct Sigma schema
Fixes applied to rule_generator.py if needed

tasks.Title: Add Download Buttons for Snort/Sigma/STIX/Markdown Files
tasks.Objective: Add download buttons in PlaybookViewer for each export format. Each format downloads as the correct file extension (.rules, .yml, .json, .md).
Tasks: Add 'Download Snort Rules' button — downloads as .rules file
Add 'Download Sigma Rules' button — downloads as .yml file
Add 'Download STIX Bundle' button — downloads as .json file
Add 'Download Playbook' button — downloads as .md file
Use Blob + URL.createObjectURL pattern for client-side downloads
Style download buttons with consistent icon + label design
Test each download format produces valid file content
Deliverables: Download buttons added to PlaybookViewer for all 4 formats
Each format downloads with correct file extension
Blob-based download implementation

tasks.Title: Add Pagination to Sentinel Playbooks API
tasks.Objective: Add pagination support to GET /api/sentinel/playbooks with page and per_page query params. Return paginated response with total count.
Tasks: Add ?page=1&per_page=20 query parameter support to GET /api/sentinel/playbooks
Calculate total count, page offset, and per_page limit in DB query
Return paginated response: { total, page, per_page, playbooks[] }
Add validation for page (>= 1) and per_page (1-100) params
Default to page=1, per_page=20 when not specified
Update existing API tests to verify pagination works
Test with various page sizes and edge cases (empty results, last page)
Deliverables: GET /api/sentinel/playbooks supports ?page=&per_page= params
Paginated response format: { total, page, per_page, playbooks[] }
Updated API tests

tasks.Title: Add Severity-Based Snort Rule Priority Mapping
tasks.Objective: Map playbook severity levels to Snort rule priority values. CRITICAL → priority:1, HIGH → priority:2, MEDIUM → priority:3.
Tasks: Add severity-to-priority mapping in rule_generator.py
Map CRITICAL severity → Snort priority:1
Map HIGH severity → Snort priority:2
Map MEDIUM severity → Snort priority:3
Map LOW severity → Snort priority:4 (informational)
Map severity to appropriate Snort classtype values
Update existing Snort rule generation tests for new priority logic
Verify generated rules have correct priority for each severity level
Deliverables: Severity-to-priority mapping in rule_generator.py
Updated Snort rules include correct priority based on severity
Updated tests verifying priority mapping

tasks.Title: Add Timestamp and Event Count to Playbook Context
tasks.Objective: Enrich playbook context with time range and event count from campaign cluster data. Display as '147 events detected between 08:15 and 08:47 UTC'.
Tasks: Extract time range (first_seen, last_seen) from campaign cluster data
Extract event count from campaign cluster data
Add time_range and event_count to playbook template context
Update Jinja2 templates to display formatted time range and event count
Format display as 'N events detected between HH:MM and HH:MM UTC'
Update all 4 attack-pattern templates (brute_force, sqli, port_scan, data_exfiltration)
Test rendering with various time ranges and event counts
Deliverables: Playbook templates show time range and event count from cluster data
Formatted display: 'N events detected between HH:MM and HH:MM UTC'
All 4 templates updated

tasks.Title: Add Sorting to Playbook List — Date, Severity, Status
tasks.Objective: Add column header click-to-sort functionality on the playbook list. Support sorting by date, severity, and status. Default: newest first.
Tasks: Add clickable sort headers to playbook list (Date, Severity, Status columns)
Implement client-side sort for date (newest first default)
Implement client-side sort for severity (CRITICAL → HIGH → MEDIUM → LOW)
Implement client-side sort for status (Draft → Approved → Rejected)
Add sort direction indicator (▲/▼ arrow) on active column
Toggle ascending/descending on repeated clicks
Persist default sort (newest first) on initial load
Deliverables: Sortable column headers on playbook list
Client-side sorting by date, severity, status
Sort direction indicators (▲/▼)

tasks.Title: Code Review All Sentinel Files for Consistency
tasks.Objective: Review all sentinel/ files for consistency. Ensure every function has docstrings, type hints, error handling, and logging.
Tasks: Review sentinel/mitre_mapper.py — verify docstrings, type hints, logger calls
Review sentinel/rule_generator.py — verify docstrings, type hints, error handling
Review sentinel/playbook_generator.py — verify docstrings, type hints, logger calls
Review sentinel/stix_enhanced.py — verify docstrings, type hints, error handling
Review sentinel/sentinel_service.py — verify docstrings, type hints, logger calls on errors
Review sentinel/models.py — verify docstrings, column comments
Review api/sentinel.py — verify docstrings, error handling, response models
Fix any missing docstrings, type hints, or error handling across all files
Deliverables: All sentinel/ functions have docstrings
All sentinel/ functions have type hints on signatures
Logger calls on all error paths
Consistent error handling patterns across all files
Warnings: This is a review + fix task — apply fixes directly, don't just document issues.
Do NOT change functional behavior — consistency and documentation only.

tasks.Title: Code Review Rule Generator Edge Cases
tasks.Objective: Review and handle edge cases in rule_generator.py: IPv6 addresses, port 0, empty attack_desc, very long IPs. Add input validation.
Tasks: Test rule generation with IPv6 addresses — handle or reject gracefully
Test rule generation with port 0 — handle or return error
Test rule generation with empty attack_desc — provide default description
Test rule generation with very long IP strings — validate max length
Add input validation function to reject invalid inputs early
Return error dict if input is invalid instead of generating malformed rules
Add unit tests for each edge case
Document edge case handling behavior
Deliverables: Edge case handling in rule_generator.py
Input validation function with clear error returns
Unit tests for edge cases (IPv6, port 0, empty desc, long IPs)

tasks.Title: Code Review Playbook Templates for Completeness
tasks.Objective: Review every .j2 template file to ensure all 7 sections are filled with no empty blocks. Manual review of each template.
Tasks: Review base_playbook.md.j2 — verify all 7 sections (Header, Summary, IOC Table, ATT&CK Mapping, Containment Steps, Artifacts, metadata)
Review brute_force.md.j2 — verify specific containment steps (key rotation, auth log review)
Review sqli_attempt.md.j2 — verify specific steps (WAF review, input validation, DB integrity)
Review port_scan.md.j2 — verify specific steps (network segmentation, exposed service audit)
Review data_exfiltration.md.j2 — verify specific steps (DLP review, file integrity, outbound traffic)
Ensure no Jinja2 blocks render as empty when data is provided
Fix any incomplete or empty sections found
Test each template with mock data to verify rendering
Deliverables: All 5 Jinja2 templates reviewed and verified complete
All 7 sections render correctly with no empty blocks
Fixes applied to any incomplete templates

tasks.Title: Add Notification Badge to Sentinel Nav Link
tasks.Objective: Add a small badge/counter next to the Sentinel nav link showing the count of pending (draft) playbooks. Fetch from /api/sentinel/stats on Navbar mount.
Tasks: Add useEffect to Navbar component to fetch /api/sentinel/stats on mount
Extract draft/pending playbook count from stats response
Display small badge/counter next to the Sentinel nav link
Style badge to be consistent with existing Navbar design
Badge should only appear when count > 0
Update badge count when navigating back to dashboard
Handle API errors gracefully (hide badge if stats unavailable)
Deliverables: Notification badge on Sentinel nav link showing draft count
Badge fetches from /api/sentinel/stats via useEffect
Badge hidden when count is 0 or API unavailable
Warnings: The Sentinel nav link was added in Week 13 Day 1. Do NOT recreate it — only add the badge.
Use the existing FaShieldAlt icon and navLinks array structure.

tasks.Title: Full System Integration Test — All Services
tasks.Objective: Start all services, trigger attacks, verify full pipeline: SSH brute force → ML scores → campaign clustered → sentinel playbook generated → visible on dashboard → approve → export.
Tasks: Start backend (uvicorn), frontend (npm run dev), and honeypots
Trigger SSH brute force attack simulation against honeypot
Verify ML threat analyzer scores the traffic
Verify campaign clustering groups the attack events
Verify sentinel generates a playbook with correct ATT&CK mapping
Verify playbook is visible on Sentinel Dashboard
Verify approve/reject buttons work on the playbook
Verify export downloads correct file formats (Snort, Sigma, STIX, Markdown)
Document the full pipeline run with timestamps and evidence
Deliverables: Full pipeline integration test documented with evidence
All services verified working together end-to-end
Test evidence: screenshots, logs, exported files
Warnings: This is a TEAM task — coordinate with all developers.
All Week 15 Day 1-4 fixes must be merged before running this test.

tasks.Title: Document Remaining Bugs for Week 16
tasks.Objective: Document any remaining bugs found during Week 15 testing. Categorize as P0 (blocks demo), P1 (wrong output), P2 (cosmetic) for Week 16 resolution.
Tasks: Compile all bugs found during Week 15 Days 1-4 testing
Categorize bugs: P0 (blocks demo), P1 (wrong output), P2 (cosmetic)
Create a bug tracking document or GitHub issues for each bug
Include reproduction steps, expected vs actual behavior for each bug
Assign priority labels to each bug issue
Identify which bugs are blockers for Week 16 work
Create a summary list for Week 16 kickoff
Deliverables: Bug tracking document with all remaining issues categorized
P0/P1/P2 priority labels assigned to each bug
Summary list ready for Week 16 planning
Warnings: This is a TEAM task — all developers contribute bugs from their area.
P0 bugs should be escalated immediately, not deferred to Week 16.
